title: Richly Sweet Australian Shallot Bake
description: A malty, gooey, kid-friendly Australian dish featuring katsuramuki-cut shallots, this paleo-friendly bake is perfect for a weeknight dinner. It combines the natural sweetness of shallots with the richness of coconut sugar and the depth of malt syrup.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together coconut sugar, malt syrup, coconut oil, salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and paprika.
Add the katsuramuki-cut shallots to the bowl and toss to coat with the sugar mixture.
Transfer the shallots to a 9x13 inch baking dish and cover with aluminum foil.
Bake for 30 minutes, then remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve the Richly Sweet Australian Shallot Bake hot, garnished with fresh parsley or thyme if desired.
